key_insight: The paper represents signals by learned complex-plane singularities instead of dense grids, enabling interpretable, stable reconstruction of sharp transients from incomplete observations.
engineering_relevance: This offers a resolution-free generative representation that can reduce Gibbs artifacts and improve zero-shot recovery for transient-dominated engineering signals.
method: Learn a latent space of per-signal singularity configurations using a transformer-based diffusion model.
Predict singularity coordinates directly in the complex plane under geometric/physical constraints during sampling.
Reconstruct signals on arbitrary output grids without retraining or interpolation.
evidence_signal: On 1D Burgers shocks, the method uses 32 singularities per signal, preserves total variation near 1 under unseen noise, and achieves 4.2× lower zero-shot sub-resolution error than a grid baseline.
watch_item: The approach is demonstrated on a controlled 1D shock recovery task, so scalability and robustness for noisier, higher-dimensional real-world signals still need validation.
paper_title: The Singularity Space: A Generative Diffusion Framework for Signal Representation
